question:
Why do social psychologists argue that "the halo effect" — assuming someone is good in all ways because they excel in one — is a problematic basis for judgment?
A) It exclusively benefits unattractive people.
B) One positive trait is legally sufficient evidence of overall character.
C) It causes us to overlook real flaws and grant unearned trust based on a single, often superficial, characteristic.
D) The halo effect has been experimentally disproven and no longer applies.
